blocks:
Surah Ya Sin

It is Meccan according to everyone, except for one verse from it, which is His saying: وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَنْفِقُوا* (And when they are told, 'Spend [in Allah's way]...) *It was revealed in Medina. Its number of verses is eighty-three verses; (it is) eighty-two according to the Kufans, (and eighty-three) in the remaining (counts). بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يس ** (1) Ya Sin. Its parallels have already passed. And it was said that its meaning is 'O man' (يا إنسان) in the language of the tribe of Tayy (طيّ).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): As for يس ** (Ya Sin), it is a name among the names of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and its meaning is 'O listener to the revelation' (يا أيّها السّامع للوحي).

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم has ten names: five in the Quran and five that are not in the Quran. As for those that are in the Quran, they are Muhammad, Ahmad, Abdullah, يس ** (Ya Sin), and ن ** (Nun).

In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): This (name) Muhammad, they were permitted to name (their children) with it. So who permitted them (to name with) **يس ** (Ya Sin)? Meaning, the naming (itself), and it is a name of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): In a narration of his in the assembly of al-Ma'mun, he said: "Inform me about the saying of Allah, the Almighty: يس وَالْقُرْآنِ الْحَكِيمِ إِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** (Ya Sin. By the wise Quran, You are indeed one of the apostles, on a straight path.) Whom did He mean by His saying يس ** (Ya Sin)?" The scholars said: "يس ** (Ya Sin) is Muhammad; no one doubts this." (This is part of) the hadith, the entirety of which has already preceded in Surah al-Ahzab regarding His saying, the Almighty: صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً ** (Invoke blessings on him and greet him with a [worthy] greeting.) And it will also come in Surah al-Saffat with another hadith concerning the argumentation (الاحتجاج) regarding that, if Allah wills.

In al-Majalis, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: سَلامٌ عَلى إِلْ ياسِينَ ** (Peace be to Ilyasin!) He said: "يس ** (Ya Sin) is Muhammad, and we are the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد)."

(٢) وَالْقُرْآنِ الْحَكِيمِ ** (2) By the wise Quran, The waw (الواو) is for oath (القسم). (٣) إِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ. (3) you are indeed one of the apostles, (٤) عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** (4) on a straight path. And it is al-Tawhid (التوحيد) and uprightness (الاستقامة) in affairs (الأمور).

And القمّيّ said, Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: يس ** is the name of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And the proof for that is the Almighty's saying: إِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ . He said: [meaning] upon the clear path (الطريق الواضح).

(٥) تَنْزِيلَ الْعَزِيزِ الرَّحِيمِ ** (5) a sending down from the All-mighty, the All-merciful. He said: The Quran. And it was recited with the rafʿ (الرفع) (nominative case). (٦) لِتُنْذِرَ قَوْماً ما أُنْذِرَ آبَاؤُهُمْ فَهُمْ غافِلُونَ (١) (6) so that you may warn a people whose fathers were not warned, so they are heedless. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: لِتُنْذِرَ ** (so that you may warn) refers to the people among whom you are, just as their fathers were warned آباؤُهُمْ فَهُمْ غافِلُونَ ** (whose fathers were not warned, so they are heedless) about Allah, and about His Messenger, and about His threat (وعيده).

(7) لَقَدْ حَقَّ الْقَوْلُ عَلى أَكْثَرِهِمْ ** (The word has already become due against most of them) He said: [This refers to] those who do not affirm the wilāyah (ولاية) of Ali, the Commander of the Faithful, and the Imams (peace be upon them) after him. فَهُمْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ (so they will not believe) He said: [They do not believe] in the Imamate (إمامة) of the Commander of the Faithful and the Successors (الأوصياء) (peace be upon them) after him. So, when they did not affirm [this], their punishment was what Allah mentioned.

** إِنَّا جَعَلْنا فِي أَعْناقِهِمْ أَغْلالاً فَهِيَ إِلَى الْأَذْقانِ فَهُمْ مُقْمَحُونَ ** (8) We have put shackles on their necks, reaching up to the chins, so that their heads are upturned. Al-Qummi (said): They have raised their heads. ** وَجَعَلْنا مِنْ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ سَدًّا وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ سَدًّا فَأَغْشَيْناهُمْ فَهُمْ لا يُبْصِرُونَ (9) We have put a barrier before them and a barrier behind them, and We have blindfolded them, so they do not see. Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: "So We blinded them, and they do not see the guidance. Allah took their hearing, their sight, and their hearts, and blinded them from the guidance." And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "This is in the world (الدّنيا), and in the Hereafter, they will be upturned (مقمحون) in the Fire of Hell." Al-Qummi (said): It was revealed concerning Abu Jahl ibn Hisham and a group of his family members. This was because the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله stood up to pray, and Abu Jahl (may Allah curse him) had sworn that if he saw him praying, he would smash his head. So he came to him, carrying a stone, while the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was standing in prayer. Every time he raised the stone to throw it, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, fixed his hand to his neck, and the stone would not turn in his hand. When he returned to his companions, the stone fell from his hand. Then another man stood up, who was also from his clan (رهطه), and said: "I will kill him." When he drew near to him, he began listening to the recitation of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and he became terrified. He returned to his companions and said: "Something intervened between me and him, like the form of a stallion (الفحل) swinging its tail. I feared to advance."

** وَسَواءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَأَنْذَرْتَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تُنْذِرْهُمْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (10) And it is the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will not believe.

He said: None of those people (الرَّهط) from Banu Makhzum believed.

And in al-Kafi, in the preceding narration: So they do not believe in Allah, nor in the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) and those after him. It was said:

**إِنَّا جَعَلْنا فِي أَعْناقِهِمْ أَغْلالاً فَهِيَ إِلَى الْأَذْقانِ فَهُمْ مُقْمَحُونَ ** (8) Indeed, We have put shackles on their necks, reaching up to the chins, so they are stiff-necked.

They have raised their heads.

**وَجَعَلْنا مِنْ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ سَدًّا ** (9) And We have put a barrier before them

The two verses are a confirmation of their resolve (تصميمهم) upon disbelief (الكفر) and the sealing (الطّبع) upon their hearts, such that the signs (الآيات) and warnings (النّذر) are of no avail. (This is done) by likening them to those whose necks are shackled, and the shackles reach up to their chins, preventing them from bowing their heads. So they are muqmahūn (مُقْمَحُونَ), raising their heads, lowering their gaze (or averting their eyes), (signifying) that they do not turn toward the truth (الحق) with a turning, nor do they bend their necks toward it, nor do they bow their heads to it. And (likening them) to those whom two barriers (سدّان) have encompassed, covering their sight such that they do not see what is before them or behind them, (signifying) that they are imprisoned in the pit (مطمورة) of ignorance (الجهالة), prevented from looking at the signs (الآيات) and proofs (الدلائل).

And saddan (سدّاً) was read with a ḍammah (بالضمّ) [i.e., suddan (سُدّاً)], and this is a linguistic variant (لغة فيه) of it.

(١١) ** إِنَّما تُنْذِرُ مَنِ اتَّبَعَ الذِّكْرَ (11) You only warn him who follows the Reminder In al-Kafi, in the preceding narrative: He means the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَخَشِيَ الرَّحْمنَ بِالْغَيْبِ فَبَشِّرْهُ بِمَغْفِرَةٍ وَأَجْرٍ كَرِيمٍ. and fears the All-beneficent in secret. So give him the good news of forgiveness and a noble reward. (١٢) ** إِنَّا نَحْنُ نُحْيِ الْمَوْتى ** (12) Indeed, it is We who revive the dead, The dead (الأموات) through resurrection (البعث) and the ignorant (الجهّال) through guidance (الهداية). وَنَكْتُبُ ما قَدَّمُوا and We write down what they have sent ahead, meaning what they have previously done (ما أسلفوا) of good and bad deeds (الأعمال الصالحة والطالحة). وَآثارَهُمْ ** and their traces. Such as knowledge they taught, or steps they walked with to the mosques, or the spreading of falsehood (إشاعة باطل) and the establishment of injustice (تأسيس ظلم). In al-Majma': The Banu Salimah were in a district of Medina, and they complained to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله about the distance of their homes from the Mosque and praying with him. So the Ayah was revealed. وَكُلَّ شَيْءٍ أَحْصَيْناهُ فِي إِمامٍ مُبِينٍ ** and We have enumerated everything in a manifest register. It is said that it means the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ). And al-Qummi means in a manifest book (كتاب مُبين). And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) that He said: "I, by Allah, am the Manifest Register (الإمام المبين). I clarify the truth from falsehood. I inherited it from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir, from his father, from his grandfather (peace be upon them), He said: When this Ayah was revealed to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: وَكُلَّ شَيْءٍ أَحْصَيْناهُ فِي إِمامٍ مُبِينٍ ** (and We have enumerated everything in a manifest register), Abu Bakr and Umar stood up from their sitting place and said: "O Messenger of Allah, is it the Torah?" He said: "No." They said: "Then is it the Gospel?" He said: "No." They said: "Then is it the Quran?" He said: "No." Then the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) approached, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "It is this one. Indeed, He is the Register (الإمام) in whom Allah has enumerated the knowledge of everything." In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله in a narrative, He said: "O assembly of people, there is no knowledge except that my Lord taught it to me, and I taught it to Ali. Allah has enumerated it in me, and every knowledge I have known, I have enumerated it in the Register of the Pious (إِمام المتّقين). And there is no knowledge except that I have taught it to Ali."

(١٣) وَاضْرِبْ لَهُمْ مَثَلاً أَصْحابَ الْقَرْيَةِ ** (Cite for them the parable of the inhabitants of the town) the town of Antioch (أنطاكية). *إِذْ جاءَهَا الْمُرْسَلُونَ قيل أرسلهم الله أو أرسلهم عيسى على نبيّنا وآله وعليه السلام بأمر الله. (when the apostles came to it.) It was said that Allah sent them, or that Jesus (عيسى) sent them by the command of Allah (upon our Prophet and his progeny and upon him be peace). (١٤) إِذْ أَرْسَلْنا إِلَيْهِمُ اثْنَيْنِ فَكَذَّبُوهُما فَعَزَّزْنا ** (When We sent to them two [apostles], and they denied them, We reinforced [them]) meaning: We strengthened [them], بِثالِثٍ ** (with a third.) who was Sham'un (شمعون). فَقالُوا إِنَّا إِلَيْكُمْ مُرْسَلُونَ. (Then they said, 'We have been sent to you.') In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), that he was asked about the interpretation of this verse, and he said: Allah sent two men to the people of the city of Antioch (أنطاكية). They came to them with what they did not recognize, so they treated them harshly, seized them, and imprisoned them in the house of idols (بيت الأصنام). Then Allah sent the third man, who entered the city and said: "Guide me to the king's gate." He said: When he stood at the gate, he said: "I am a man who used to worship (أَتَعَيَّدُ) in a desolate land, and I have desired to worship the god of the king." They conveyed his words to the king, who said: "Bring him into the house of the deities." So they brought him in, and he stayed for a year with his two companions. He said to them: "Is this how people are moved from one religion to another, through harshness (بالخرق)? Why were you not gentle?" Then he said to them: "Do you not acknowledge my acquaintance?" Then he entered upon the king. The king said to him: "I have been informed that you used to worship my god, so you are my brother. Ask me for your need." He said: "I have no need, O King, but I saw two men in the house of the deities. What is their situation?" The king said: "These are two men who came to me with the falsehood of my religion and invite me to a heavenly God." He said: "O King, let there be a beautiful debate (مناظرة جميلة). If the truth is with them, we will follow them, and if the truth is with us, they will enter our religion with us, and they will have what we have and owe what we owe." He said: So the king sent for them. When they entered upon him, their companion said to them: "What is it that you have brought me?" They said: "We have come inviting him to the worship of Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, and Who creates in the wombs what He wills, and fashions how He wills, and caused the trees and fruits to grow, and sent down rain (القطر) from the sky." He said: Then he said to them: "If we bring a blind man, can this God of yours, to Whom you invite and to Whose worship you call, restore his sight?" They said: "If we ask Him to do so, He will do it, if He wills." He said: "O King, bring me a blind man who has never seen anything at all." He said: So he was brought. He said to them: "Call upon your God to restore the sight of this man." So they stood up and prayed two units (ركعتين), and behold, his eyes were open, and he was looking at the sky. He said: "O King, bring me another blind man." So he was brought. He said: He prostrated once (سجدة) and then raised his head, and behold, the blind man could see. He said: "O King, a proof for a proof (حجّة بحجّة). Bring me a paralyzed man (مقعد)." So he was brought. He said the same thing to them. So they prayed and supplicated Allah, and behold, the paralyzed man's legs were released, and he stood up and walked. He said: "O King, bring me another paralyzed man." So he was brought, and he did with him as he had done the first time, and the paralyzed man walked away. He said: "O King, they have brought two proofs, and we have brought their like. But one thing remains. If they do it, I will enter their religion with them." Then he said: "O King, I have been informed that the king had one son who died. If their God revives him, I will enter their religion with them." The king said to him: "I am with you too." Then he said to them: "This one thing remains: the king's son has died. Call upon your God to revive him." He said: So they fell prostrate to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ*), and prolonged the prostration. Then they raised their heads and said to the king: "Send to the grave of your son; you will find that he has risen from his grave, if Allah wills." He said: So the people went out to look, and they found him having emerged from his grave, shaking the dust from his head. He said: He was brought to the king, who recognized that he was his son. He said: "What is your situation, my son?" He said: "I was dead, and I saw two men before my Lord right now, prostrating, asking Him to revive me, and He revived me." He said: "My son, do you recognize them if you see them?" He said: "Yes." He said: So the people were taken out collectively to the desert. A man would pass by him, and his father would say to him: "Look," and he would say: "No." Then, after a large crowd, one of the two men passed by him, and he said: "This is one of them," and he pointed to him with his hand. Then many other people passed by until he saw his other companion, and he said: "And this is the other." He said: Then the Prophet (peace be upon him), the companion of the two men, said: "As for me, I have believed in your God, and I know that what you have brought is the truth." He said: Then the king said: "And I too have believed in your God," and all the people of his kingdom believed.

In al-Majma', Wahb ibn Munabbih (وهب بن منبه) said: Jesus (عيسى) sent these two apostles to Antioch (أنطاكية). They came to it but did not reach its king. Their stay was prolonged, and one day the king went out, and they magnified Allah and mentioned Him. He became angry and ordered them to be imprisoned and that each of them be lashed one hundred times. When the two apostles were denied and beaten, Jesus (peace be upon him) sent Sham'un al-Safa (شمعون الصّفا), the chief of the disciples (رأس الحواريّين), after them to support them. Sham'un entered the town disguised (منكّراً). He began to associate with the king's entourage until they became familiar with him. They conveyed news of him to the king, who summoned him, was pleased with his company, became familiar with him, and honored him. Then, one day, he said to him: "O King, I have been informed that you imprisoned two men and beat them when they invited you to a religion other than yours. Did you hear what they had to say?" The king said: "The state of anger prevented me from doing so." He said: "If the King deems it fit, summon them so that he may examine what they possess." So the king summoned them. Sham'un said to them: "Who sent you here?" They said: "Allah, Who created everything and has no partner." He said: "And what did He grant you?" They said: "Whatever you wish." So the king commanded that a boy whose eyes were obliterated (مطموس العينين), the place of his eyes being like his forehead, be brought. They continued to supplicate Allah until the place of sight split open. They took two pellets (بندقتين) of clay and placed them in his eye sockets, and they became two eyeballs (مقلتين) (1) with which he could see. The king was astonished. Sham'un said to the king: "Tell me, what if you asked your god to perform a deed like this, so that honor may be for you and your god?" The king said: "I have no secret from you: our god whom we worship neither harms nor benefits." Then the king said to the two apostles: "If your God is capable of reviving the dead, we will believe in Him and in you." They said: "Our God is capable of all things." The king said: "There is a dead man here who died seven days ago. We have not buried him until his father, who was absent, returns." So they brought the dead man, who had changed and begun to smell (أروح). They began to supplicate their Lord openly, while Sham'un supplicated his Lord secretly. The dead man stood up and said to them: "I died seven days ago and was admitted into seven valleys of the Fire. I warn you against what you are in, so believe in Allah." The king was astonished. When Sham'un realized that his words had affected the king, he invited him to Allah, and he believed. Some of the people of his kingdom believed, and others disbelieved. Al-'Ayyashi (العيّاشي) narrated something similar with his chain of transmission from al-Thumali (الثمالي) and others, from Abu Ja'far and Abu 'Abdillah (peace be upon them), except that in some narrations: Allah sent the two apostles to Antioch (أنطاكية), then sent the third; and in some of them: Allah revealed to Jesus (peace be upon him) that He should send them, and then He sent his successor (وصيّه), Sham'un, to rescue them; and that the dead man whom Allah revived through their supplication was the king's son, and that he emerged from his grave shaking the dust from his head. He said to him: "My son, what is your situation?" He said: "I was dead, and I saw two men prostrating, asking Allah to revive me." He said: "My son, do you recognize them if you see them?" He said: "Yes." So the people were taken out to the desert. A man would pass by him after another. One of them passed by after a large crowd, and he said: "This is one of them." Then the other passed by, and he recognized them and pointed to them with his hand. So the king and the people of his kingdom believed. The words of the author of al-Majma' end here.

footnote (١) The *muqlah* (*المقلة*) is the eyeball that encompasses the black and white parts of the eye.

(١٥) قالُوا ما أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُنا ** (15) They said, 'You are nothing but humans like us, You have no distinction (مزيّة) over us that necessitates your being singled out for what you claim. وَما أَنْزَلَ الرَّحْمنُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** and the All-beneficent has not sent down anything. (meaning) revelation (وحي) or message (رسالة). إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا تَكْذِبُونَ ** You are only lying.' regarding the claim of His message (رسالته).

(١٦) قالُوا رَبُّنا يَعْلَمُ إِنَّا إِلَيْكُمْ لَمُرْسَلُونَ ** (16) They said, 'Our Lord knows that we have indeed been sent to you.' The seeking of testimony by Allah's knowledge runs in the manner of an oath (القسم). (١٧) وَما عَلَيْنا إِلَّا الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ. (17) Our duty is only to communicate in clear terms.' (١٨) قالُوا إِنَّا تَطَيَّرْنا بِكُمْ ** (18) They said, 'We augur ill of you. If you do not desist, we will surely stone you, and a painful punishment will surely befall you from us.' We augur ill of you (تشاءمنا بكم). It was said that this was due to their finding strange what they claimed by it and their aversion from it. And al-Qummi said regarding تَطَيَّرْنا بِكُمْ : He said: by your names. لَئِنْ لَمْ تَنْتَهُوا : from this saying of yours. لَنَرْجُمَنَّكُمْ وَلَيَمَسَّنَّكُمْ مِنَّا عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ.

(١٩) ** قالُوا طائِرُكُمْ مَعَكُمْ ** (They said, 'Your ill omen is with yourselves) The cause of your ill omen is with you, and that is the badness of your belief (عقيدتكم) and your deeds (أعمالكم). أَإِنْ ذُكِّرْتُمْ ** (Is it because you were reminded?) Is it that when you are admonished by it, you take an ill omen, or you threaten with stoning (الرّجم) and torment (التعذيب)? Thus, the answer (to the conditional clause) is omitted. بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ مُسْرِفُونَ ** (Rather, you are an extravagant people.') Your custom is extravagance (الإسراف).

** وَجاءَ مِنْ أَقْصَا الْمَدِينَةِ رَجُلٌ يَسْعى قالَ يا قَوْمِ اتَّبِعُوا الْمُرْسَلِينَ. (20) And a man came running from the farthest end of the city. He said, 'O my people! Follow the apostles!' Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning حبيب النجّار (Habib al-Najjar) up to His saying: وَجَعَلَنِي مِنَ الْمُكْرَمِينَ ** (and He made me one of the honored ones). It was said that he was among those who believed in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and there were six hundred years between them. And it was said that he was in a cave worshipping Allah, and when the news of the Messengers reached him, he manifested his faith (دينه). In al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said: "The truthful ones (الصدّيقون) are three: حبيب النجّار (Habib al-Najjar), the believer of the people of يس (آل يس), who says: اتَّبِعُوا الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** (Follow the apostles!)—the Ayah—and حزقيل (Hizqil), the believer of the people of Pharaoh (آل فرعون), and عليّ بن أبي طالب (Ali ibn Abi Talib) عليه السلام, and he is the most excellent of them." In al-Jawami', narrated from Him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said: "The foremost ones (سبّاق) of the nations are three who never disbelieved in Allah for the blink of an eye: عليّ بن أبي طالب (Ali ibn Abi Talib) عليه السلام, the Companion of يس (صاحب يس), and the believer of the people of Pharaoh (مؤمن آل فرعون). They are the truthful ones (الصدّيقون), and عليّ (Ali) is the most excellent of them." In al-Khisal, narrated from Him عليه السلام, He said: "Three never disbelieved in the Revelation (الوحي) for the blink of an eye: the believer of the people of يس (مؤمن آل يس), عليّ بن أبي طالب (Ali ibn Abi Talib) عليه السلام, and آسية (Asiyah), the wife of Pharaoh."

(٢١) اتَّبِعُوا مَنْ لا يَسْئَلُكُمْ أَجْراً ** (21) Follow those who do not ask you any reward for the advice (النّصح) and the conveyance of the message (تبليغ الرّسالة) وَهُمْ مُهْتَدُونَ ** (21) and they are rightly guided. to the good of both abodes (الدارين). (٢٢) وَما لِيَ لا أَعْبُدُ الَّذِي فَطَرَنِي ** (22) Why should I not worship Him who originated me? He showed gentleness (تلطّف) in guidance (الإرشاد) by presenting it in the context of advising himself (المناصحة لنفسه) and making the advice sincere (إمحاض النصح) since he desired for them what he desired for himself. And the intent (المراد) is to reproach them (تقريعهم) for abandoning the worship of their Creator (خالقهم) for the worship of other than Him. And for that reason, He said: وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (and to Him you shall be brought back) as an exaggeration in the threat (مبالغة في التهديد). Then he returned to the first course (المساق الأوّل) and said.

(٢٣) أَأَتَّخِذُ مِنْ دُونِهِ آلِهَةً إِنْ يُرِدْنِ الرَّحْمنُ بِضُرٍّ لا تُغْنِ عَنِّي شَفاعَتُهُمْ شَيْئاً ** (23) Shall I take gods besides Him? If the All-beneficent desires to cause me some distress, their intercession will not avail me in the least, Their intercession will not benefit me وَلا يُنْقِذُونِ ** nor will they save me, through aid (النّصر) and support (المظاهرة). (٢٤) إِنِّي إِذاً لَفِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ ** (24) In that case, I shall indeed be in manifest error. Clear (بيّن), which is not hidden from any rational person (عاقل). (٢٥) إِنِّي آمَنْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ ** (25) Indeed, I have faith in your Lord, Who created you, or this is an address to the Messengers after the people intended to kill him, فَاسْمَعُونِ (so listen to me), so listen to my faith. (٢٦) قِيلَ ادْخُلِ الْجَنَّةَ ** (26) He was told, 'Enter paradise.' That was said to him when they killed him, as glad tidings that he was one of the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة), or as an honor (اكراماً) and permission (اذنا) for him to enter it. قالَ يا لَيْتَ قَوْمِي يَعْلَمُونَ. (27) He said, 'Alas! Would that my people knew (٢٧) بِما غَفَرَ لِي رَبِّي وَجَعَلَنِي مِنَ الْمُكْرَمِينَ. (27) for what my Lord has forgiven me and made me one of the honored ones!' In al-Jawami', it is narrated in a marfu' tradition: that he advised his people both alive and dead. (٢٨) وَما أَنْزَلْنا عَلى قَوْمِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ مِنْ جُنْدٍ مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (28) We did not send down upon his people after him any host from the sky, To destroy them, as We sent down on the Day of Badr and the Trench (الخندق). Rather, We sufficed their affair with a single cry (صيحة). وَما كُنَّا مُنْزِلِينَ ** (nor were We going to send down [any]). And it was not correct according to Our wisdom (حكمتنا) that We should send down [a host], since We had decreed a means (سبباً) for everything, and We made that [single cry] a means for your victory over your people. And it is said that ما (mā) is a relative pronoun (موصولة) coordinated with جُنْدٍ (host), meaning: وَما كُنَّا مُنْزِلِينَ ** (nor were We going to send down) upon those before them, such as stones, wind, and severe rains (أمطار شديدة).

(٢٩) ** إِنْ كانَتْ ** It was not, meaning the seizing (الأخذة), ** إِلَّا صَيْحَةً واحِدَةً ** except a single cry, which Gabriel (peace be upon him) cried out, ** فَإِذا هُمْ خامِدُونَ ** and behold, they were extinguished. Meaning: dead. They were likened to fire, symbolizing that the living is like the blazing fire and the dead is like its ashes.

(٣٠) ** يا حَسْرَةً عَلَى الْعِبادِ ** O Regret, come forth, for this is your time. In al-Jawami', narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The phrase يا حَسْرَة الْعِبادِ (Alas, the regret of the servants) is due to the attribution to them, because it is specific to them, in that it is directed towards them: ** ما يَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلَّا كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** (No apostle comes to them but they ridicule him.) For those who ridicule the sincere advisors, upon whose advice rests the good of the two abodes (الدّارين), are deserving that they regret, and that regret be felt for them. And the angels and the believers from both groups (الثقلين) have indeed grieved over their condition.

(٣١) أَلَمْ يَرَوْا كَمْ أَهْلَكْنا قَبْلَهُمْ مِنَ الْقُرُونِ أَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ لا يَرْجِعُونَ. (31) Do they not see how many generations We destroyed before them, who will not return to them?

(٣٢) وَإِنْ كُلٌّ لَمَّا جَمِيعٌ لَدَيْنا مُحْضَرُونَ ** (32) And all of them will indeed be brought before Us. In (إن) is lightened (مخفّفة) from the heavy (الثّقيلة) [form, إنّ], and mā (ما) is added for emphasis (للتّأكيد). And lammā (لمّا) was read with tashdīd (بالتشديد) [on the mīm] meaning "except" (الّا), in which case in (إن) is negative (نافية).

(٣٣) وَآيَةٌ لَهُمُ الْأَرْضُ الْمَيْتَةُ ** (33) A sign for them is the dead earth, which We revive and bring forth grain from it, whereof they eat. And it was read with tashdīd (بالتشديد) [on the yā’ of الميتة]. أَحْيَيْناها وَأَخْرَجْنا مِنْها حَبًّا فَمِنْهُ يَأْكُلُونَ It was said that the relative clause (الصلة) was brought forward to indicate that grain (الحبّ) is the majority of what is eaten and lived upon.

(٣٤) وَجَعَلْنا فِيها جَنَّاتٍ مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَأَعْنابٍ وَفَجَّرْنا فِيها مِنَ الْعُيُونِ. (34) And We made in it gardens of date-palms and vines, and We caused springs to gush forth in it,

(٣٥) لِيَأْكُلُوا مِنْ ثَمَرِهِ ** (35) so that they may eat of its fruit, and what their hands have not wrought. Will they not then be thankful? The fruit (ثمر) of what was mentioned, and it was read with two ḍammahs (بضمّتين) [i.e., ثُمُرِهِ]. وَما عَمِلَتْهُ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** from what is made from it, such as juice (العصير), molasses (الدّبس), and their likes. And it was read without the hā’ (بلا هاء) [i.e., وَمَا عَمِلَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ]. And it was said that mā (ما) is negative (نافية). أَفَلا يَشْكُرُونَ.

(٣٦) سُبْحانَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ الْأَزْواجَ كُلَّها ** (36) Immaculate is He who created all the pairs of what the earth grows, and of themselves, and of what they do not know. The types (الأنواع) and the varieties (الأصناف). مِمَّا تُنْبِتُ الْأَرْضُ ** from plants (النبات) and trees (الشجر). وَمِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** the male (الذكر) and the female (الأنثى). وَمِمَّا لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** And pairs (أزواجاً) from what Allah does not inform them of.

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Verily, the seminal fluid (النطفة) falls from the heaven to the earth upon the plants (النبات), the fruit (الثمر), and the trees (الشجر). Then people and livestock (البهائم) eat from it, and it flows within them.

(٣٧) ** وَآيَةٌ لَهُمُ اللَّيْلُ نَسْلَخُ مِنْهُ النَّهارَ ** (37) And a sign for them is the night: We strip the day from it We remove it and uncover its place. [This is] borrowed from the skinning of the sheep. ** فَإِذا هُمْ مُظْلِمُونَ ** then behold, they are plunged in darkness. entering into the darkness.

In al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the passing of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the darkness appeared, so they did not perceive the virtue of His Household (أهل بيته).

(٣٨) وَالشَّمْسُ تَجْرِي لِمُسْتَقَرٍّ لَها ** (38) And the sun runs on to a resting place for it. To a specific limit where its cycle terminates. And in al-Majma', narrated from the two of them (peace be upon them): [The reading is] lā mustaqarra lahā (with the rā' having a fatḥah), meaning there is no stillness for it, for it is constantly moving. ذلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ.** (38) That is the ordinance of the All-mighty, the All-knowing.

(٣٩) وَالْقَمَرَ ** (39) and the moon— And it was read with the accusative case (بالنّصب) قَدَّرْناهُ ** We have determined its stations, We determined its course as stations (مَنازِلَ), and they are twenty-eight stations (منزلاً). It descends every night in one of them, neither overstepping it nor falling short of it, حَتَّى عادَ كَالْعُرْجُونِ الْقَدِيمِ ** until it returns like an old date stalk. Like the bent (المعوج) old (العتيق) date stalk (كالشمراخ).

(40) ** لَا الشَّمْسُ يَنْبَغِي لَها ** It is proper for it and made easy **أَنْ تُدْرِكَ الْقَمَرَ وَلَا اللَّيْلُ سابِقُ النَّهارِ وَكُلٌّ فِي فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ ** they travel in it smoothly. (40) The sun is not allowed to overtake the moon, nor does the night outrun the day, and each swims in an orbit.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: The sun is the sovereign (سلطان) of the day, and the moon is the sovereign (سلطان) of the night. It is not proper for the sun to be with the light of the moon during the night, nor does the night precede the day. He says: The night does not depart until the day overtakes it. وَكُلٌّ فِي فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ ** He says: It comes behind the orbit (الفلك), which is the circular path (الاستدارة).

I say: Meaning, it comes following the movement of the orbit (الفلك) along the circular path (الاستدارة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Ayyashi, from al-Rida (peace be upon him): That the day was created before the night. And regarding His saying, the Almighty: **وَلَا اللَّيْلُ سابِقُ النَّهارِ ** He said: Meaning, the day preceded it.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The day was created before the night, and the sun before the moon, and the earth before the heaven (السماء).

And he added in al-Kafi: And the light (النّور) was created before the darkness (الظلمة).

(٤١)** وَآيَةٌ لَهُمْ أَنَّا حَمَلْنا ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ فِي الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ ** (41) And a sign for them is that We carried their progeny in the laden ark.

The meaning of الْمَشْحُونِ is the filled (المملو), meaning the ark of Noah (peace be upon him), as in His saying: ذُرِّيَّةَ مَنْ حَمَلْنا مَعَ نُوحٍ ** (The progeny of those whom We carried with Noah). And Allah carrying their progeny therein was His carrying their ancient fathers, and their progenies were in their loins (أصلابهم). The specification of the progeny (الذرّية) is because it is more expressive of the favor (الامتنان) and more conducive to wonder (التعجّب), along with conciseness (الإيجاز).

In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith: He was asked about the ninety (التّسعون). He said: The laden ark (الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ). Noah made ninety compartments (بيتاً) in it for the livestock (لِلْبَهَائِمِ).

And it was said that their progeny (ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ) means their children whom they send out for their trades, or their children and women whom they take along with them, for the term الذرّية applies to them [the women] because they are the fields (مزارعها) for it [the progeny]. And their specification is because their stability (استقرارهم) therein is more difficult (أشقّ) and their holding together (تماسكهم) therein is more wondrous (أعجب).

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: The filled ships (السفن الممتلية). And it is as if he was looking at the latter meaning due to his generalization of the ark (الفلك).

(٤٢) وَخَلَقْنا لَهُمْ مِنْ مِثْلِهِ ** (And We created for them the like of it) from the like of the ark (الفلك) ما يَرْكَبُونَ ** (that which they ride) from the livestock (الأنعام) and beasts (الدوابّ), especially the camels (الإبل), for they are the ships of the land (سفائن البر), or from the ships (السفن) and small boats (الزوارق). (٤٣) وَإِنْ نَشَأْ (١) نُغْرِقْهُمْ فَلا صَرِيخَ لَهُمْ ** (And if We wish We drown them, and there is no one to rescue them) then there is no helper (مغيث) for them who protects them from drowning ** وَلا هُمْ يُنْقَذُونَ ** (nor will they be saved) they are saved (ينجون) by it from death. (٤٤) إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِنَّا وَمَتاعاً ** (except by a mercy from Us and an enjoyment) except for a mercy, and so that they may enjoy life إِلى حِينٍ ** (for a while) a time (زمان) appointed for their appointed terms (لآجالهم). (٤٥) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اتَّقُوا ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَما خَلْفَكُمْ (When they are told, 'Beware of what is before you and what is behind you') In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is: اتَّقُوا ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِيكُمْ ** ('Beware of what is before you') from sins (الذنوب) وَما خَلْفَكُمْ ** ('and what is behind you') from punishment (العقوبة) لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ ** (so that you may be shown mercy) so that you may be hoping for the mercy of Allah. And the answer (جواب) to إذا (when) is omitted (محذوف), indicated by what follows it, as if it were said: "they turned away (اعرضوا)."

(٤٦) وَما تَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ آيَةٍ مِنْ آياتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كانُوا عَنْها مُعْرِضِينَ ** (46) And there does not come to them any sign from the signs of their Lord but they turn away from it. Because they have become accustomed to it and trained themselves upon it. (٤٧) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَنْفِقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللهُ ** (47) And when they are told, 'Spend out of what Allah has provided you,' upon your needy ones قالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (47) the faithless say لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنُطْعِمُ مَنْ لَوْ يَشاءُ اللهُ أَطْعَمَهُ ** (47) to the faithful, 'Shall we feed those whom, if Allah had wished, He would have fed them? Either mocking by means of their affirmation of Allah and their linking of matters to the will of Allah, or an illusion (a suggestion) that since Allah was able to feed them but did not feed them, then we are more entitled to that (not feeding them). And this is due to the extremity of their ignorance, for Allah provides sustenance through means, among which is urging the wealthy to feed the poor and granting them success (توفيقهم) in doing so. إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ.** (47) You are only in manifest error.'

footnote (١) Meaning **وَإِنْ نَشَأْ ** (And if We wish), when We carry them in the ships, **نُغْرِقْهُمْ ** (We drown them) by stirring up the winds and the waves.

(٤٨) وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْوَعْدُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (48) And they say, 'When will this promise be, if you are truthful?' They mean the promise of the Resurrection. (٤٩) ما يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً واحِدَةً ** (49) They do not await but a single blast, It is the first blast. تَأْخُذُهُمْ وَهُمْ يَخِصِّمُونَ ** which will seize them while they are disputing. Meaning, they are disputing in their trades and transactions, and its matter does not cross their minds, just like His saying: جاءَتْهُمُ السَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً.** (The Hour came upon them suddenly.)

(٥٠) فَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ تَوْصِيَةً وَلا إِلى أَهْلِهِمْ يَرْجِعُونَ. (50) So they will not be able to make a will, nor will they return to their families.

Al-Qummi said that this is concerning the end of time (آخر الزمان): a cry (صيحة) will be sounded among them while they are disputing in their markets, so they will all die in their places. No one will return to his dwelling, nor will he make a will (وصيّة).

And in al-Majma', in the narration (الحديث): The Hour (الساعة) will be established while two men have spread out their garment, trading, and they will not fold it up until the Hour is established. And the man raises his morsel to his mouth, and it will not reach his mouth until the Hour is established. And the man is plastering his basin (حوضه) to water his livestock, and he will not water them until the Hour is established.

(٥١) وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ ** (51) And the Trumpet will be blown أي مرّة ثانية كما يأتي في سورة الزمر فَإِذا هُمْ مِنَ الْأَجْداثِ ** meaning, a second time, as will come in Surat al-Zumar. and behold, they will be emerging from the graves من القبور إِلى رَبِّهِمْ (١) يَنْسِلُونَ ** from the graves (القبور). toward their Lord, (1) hastening. يسرعون. they hasten (يسرعون). (٥٢) قالُوا يا وَيْلَنا مَنْ بَعَثَنا مِنْ مَرْقَدِنا. (52) They will say, 'Woe to us! Who has raised us from our sleep?' في الجوامع عن عليّ عليه السلام : انّه قرئ من بعثنا على من الجارّة والمصدر هذا ما وَعَدَ الرَّحْمنُ وَصَدَقَ الْمُرْسَلُونَ. In al-Jawami', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): It was recited (مَنْ بَعَثَنَا) with the مِنْ being the particle of genitive (الجارّة) and the verbal noun (المصدر): هذا ما وَعَدَ الرَّحْمنُ وَصَدَقَ الْمُرْسَلُونَ. (This is what the All-beneficent has promised, and the apostles have spoken the truth.) القمّيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام قال : فانّ القوم كانوا في القبور فلمّا قاموا حسبوا انّهم كانوا نياماً قالُوا يا وَيْلَنا مَنْ بَعَثَنا مِنْ مَرْقَدِنا قالت الملائكة هذا ما وَعَدَ الرَّحْمنُ وَصَدَقَ الْمُرْسَلُونَ. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: Indeed, the people were in the graves (القبور), and when they stood up, they thought they had been asleep. They will say, 'Woe to us! Who has raised us from our sleep?' The angels will say: هذا ما وَعَدَ الرَّحْمنُ وَصَدَقَ الْمُرْسَلُونَ.** (This is what the All-beneficent has promised, and the apostles have spoken the truth.)

footnote (١) أي إلى الموضع الذي يحكم الله فيه ولا حكم فيه لغيره هناك. (1) Meaning, to the place where Allah judges, and where there is no judgment for anyone other than Him there.

(53) إِنْ كانَتْ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً واحِدَةً (53) It will be but a single cry, It is the final blast (النفخة الأخيرة). فَإِذا هُمْ جَمِيعٌ لَدَيْنا مُحْضَرُونَ and behold, they will all be brought before Us. Immediately upon the cry (الصيحة). And in that is the minimization (تهوين) of the matter of the Resurrection (البعث) and the Gathering (الحشر), and His independence (استغناؤه) from the means (الأسباب) upon which they rely in what they observe.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Abu Dharr (may Allah have mercy upon him) used to say in his sermon: "And what is between death and the Resurrection (البعث) is nothing but a sleep that you slept and then woke up from it." (The rest of the narrative is omitted here, indicated by الحديث).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: When Allah causes the people of the earth to die, He remains for a period like that for which Allah created creation, and like that for which He caused them to die, and multiples of that. Then He causes the people of the lowest heaven (سماء الدنيا) to die. Then He remains for a period like that for which Allah created creation, and like that for which He caused the people of the earth and the people of the lowest heaven to die, and multiples of that. Then He causes the people of the second heaven to die. Then He remains for a period like that for which Allah created creation, and like that for which He caused the people of the earth, the lowest heaven, and the second heaven to die, and multiples of that. Then He causes the people of the third heaven to die. Then He remains for a period like that for which Allah created creation, and like that for which He caused the people of the earth, the lowest heaven, the second heaven, and the third heaven to die, and multiples of that. In every heaven, it is like that, and multiples of that. Then He causes Michael (ميكائيل) to die. Then He remains for a period like that for which Allah created creation, and like all of that, and multiples of that. Then He causes Gabriel (جبرئيل) to die. Then He remains for a period like that for which Allah created creation, and like all of that, and multiples of that. Then He causes Israfil (إسرافيل) to die. Then He remains for a period like that for which Allah created creation, and like all of that, and multiples of that. Then He causes the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) to die. Then He remains for a period like that for which Allah created creation, and like all of that, and multiples of that. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), says: لِمَنِ الْمُلْكُ الْيَوْمَ (To whom does the sovereignty belong today?) Then He replies to Himself: لِلَّهِ الْواحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ (To Allah, the One, the All-subduing!) "Where are the tyrants (الجبّارون)? Where are those who claimed another god besides Me? Where are the arrogant (المتكبّرون) and their haughtiness (نخوتهم)?" Then He resurrects creation. The narrator said: I said, "Will this matter last for such a long duration?" He (peace be upon him) said: "Did you see what has already passed? Did you know about it?" I said, "No." He said: "It is likewise with this."

(٥٤) فَالْيَوْمَ لا تُظْلَمُ نَفْسٌ شَيْئاً وَلا تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ. (54) So today no soul will be wronged in the least, nor will you be requited except for what you used to do. (٥٥) إِنَّ أَصْحابَ الْجَنَّةِ الْيَوْمَ فِي شُغُلٍ فاكِهُونَ ** (55) Indeed the inhabitants of paradise are busy today, enjoying themselves. They are delighting in the bliss, and its ambiguity (the word شغل) is for magnifying the state they are in. Al-Qummi said regarding the deflowering of virgins (افتضاض العذارى): فاكِهُونَ ** (enjoying themselves), He said: They converse playfully (يفاكهون) with the women and sport with them (ويلاعبونهنّ). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are busy (شغلوا) with the deflowering of virgins (بافتضاض العذارى). He said: And their eyebrows are like crescents (كالأهلّة), and the edges of their eyes (أشفار أعينهنّ) are like the primary feathers of eagles (كقوادم النّسور).

(٥٦) هُمْ وَأَزْواجُهُمْ فِي ظِلالٍ عَلَى الْأَرائِكِ ** the decorated couches (السرر المزيّنة) مُتَّكِؤُنَ.** (56) They and their spouses will be in shades, reclining on couches.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) who said: الْأَرائِكِ ** (the couches) are the beds (السرر) upon which are the canopies (الحجال).

And from him (peace be upon him) who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: When the believer sits upon his couch, his couch shakes out of joy, in a narration, some of which has preceded at the end of Surat Fatir.

(٥٧) لَهُمْ فِيها فاكِهَةٌ وَلَهُمْ ما يَدَّعُونَ ** (57) In it they will have fruits, and they will have whatever they call for. It is said that (يَدَّعُونَ) is derived from the form ifti'āl (افتعال) of al-du'ā' (الدّعاء) (supplication/calling). And it is said that it means they wish (يتمنّون), based on their saying: "Wish (ادّع) upon me whatever you want (ما شئت)," meaning: "Wish for it (تمنّه)." And it is said that it refers to what they call for (ما يدعونه) in the world (في الدنيا) regarding Paradise (الجنّة) and its ranks (درجاتها). (٥٨) سَلامٌ قَوْلاً مِنْ رَبٍّ رَحِيمٍ ** (58) 'Peace!'—a word from an all-merciful Lord. It is said to them as a saying (قولاً) originating from His direction (من جهته), meaning that Allah sends peace (يسلّم) upon them. Al-Qummi said: The peace (السلام) from Him is security (الأمان). (٥٩) وَامْتازُوا الْيَوْمَ أَيُّهَا الْمُجْرِمُونَ ** (59) 'And stand apart today, O guilty ones!' And separate (وانفردوا) yourselves from the believers (المؤمنين). That is when the believers are led (يسار) to Paradise (الجنّة), like His Almighty saying: وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَفَرَّقُونَ. (And on the day when the Hour commences, that day they will separate.) Al-Qummi said: When Allah gathers creation on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), they will remain standing upon their feet until the sweat bridles them (يلجمهم العرق). Then they will call out: "O Lord, reckon with us, even if it is to the Fire (النّار)." He said: Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), sends winds (رياحاً) which strike between them, and a caller (مناد) calls out: وَامْتازُوا الْيَوْمَ أَيُّهَا الْمُجْرِمُونَ ** (And stand apart today, O guilty ones!) So He distinguishes (فيميّز) between them. Thus, the guilty ones (المجرمون) go to the Fire (النار), and whoever had faith (الإيمان) in his heart goes to Paradise (الجنّة).

(٦٠) أَلَمْ أَعْهَدْ إِلَيْكُمْ يا بَنِي آدَمَ أَنْ لا تَعْبُدُوا الشَّيْطانَ (60) Did I not charge you, O Children of Adam, not to worship Satan?

He made it the worship of Satan because he is the one who commands it and beautifies it. And it is established that whoever obeys the created being (المخلوق) in disobeying the Creator (الخالق) has indeed worshipped him, just as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: اتَّخَذُوا أَحْبارَهُمْ وَرُهْبانَهُمْ أَرْباباً مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (They have taken their scribes and monks as lords besides Allah), where they (the scribes and monks) made unlawful things lawful for them and made lawful things unlawful for them, and they obeyed them. And whoever worships other than the Creator has worshipped his own desire (هواه), just as the Almighty Allah said: أَفَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلهَهُ هَواهُ (Have you seen him who takes his desire as his god?). And whoever worships his desire (هواه) has worshipped Satan.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever obeys a man in his disobedience (to Allah) has indeed worshipped him." And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Whoever listens attentively (أصغى) to a speaker (ناطق) has indeed worshipped him. If the speaker narrates from Allah, then he has worshipped Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and if the speaker narrates from Satan, then he has worshipped Satan."

إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ. (He is indeed an obvious enemy to you.)

(٦١) وَأَنِ اعْبُدُونِي هذا صِراطٌ مُسْتَقِيمٌ ** (61) and that, ‘Worship Me. This is a straight path.’ This is a reference to what He entrusted to them, or to the worship of Allah. (٦٢) وَلَقَدْ أَضَلَّ مِنْكُمْ جِبِلًّا كَثِيراً ** (62) Certainly he has led astray a great many of you. Meaning, a great many creatures (خَلْقاً كَثِيراً). And in it (جِبِلًّا) there are multiple variant readings (لُغَاتٌ مُتَعَدِّدَةٌ) and it was recited with them. أَفَلَمْ تَكُونُوا تَعْقِلُونَ. (Were you not sensible?) (٦٣) هذِهِ جَهَنَّمُ الَّتِي كُنْتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ. (63) This is hell, which you were promised. (٦٤) اصْلَوْهَا الْيَوْمَ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ ** (64) Enter it today because of what you used to disbelieve. Taste its heat today because of your disbelief in the world. (٦٥) الْيَوْمَ نَخْتِمُ عَلى أَفْواهِهِمْ ** (65) Today We will seal their mouths, We will prevent them from speaking. وَتُكَلِّمُنا أَيْدِيهِمْ وَتَشْهَدُ أَرْجُلُهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ. and their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will bear witness to what they used to earn. Al-Qummi said: When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, gathers the creation on the Day of Resurrection, the book (كِتَابَهُ) of every human being will be handed to him, and they will look into it and deny that they performed any of those deeds. Then the angels will bear witness against them, and they will say: "O Lord, Your angels bear witness for You." Then they will swear that they did not perform any of those deeds. This is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ جَمِيعاً فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُ كَما يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ (The day Allah raises them all, they will swear to Him, just as they swear to you). When they do that, Allah will seal their tongues, and their limbs (جَوَارِحُهُمْ) will speak concerning what they used to earn. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The limbs (الجَوَارِحُ) do not bear witness against a believer; rather, they bear witness against the one upon whom the word of punishment (كَلِمَةُ العَذَابِ) has been confirmed. As for the believer, he will be given his book (كِتَابَهُ) in his right hand. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَمَنْ أُوتِيَ كِتابَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ فَأُولئِكَ يَقْرَؤُنَ كِتابَهُمْ وَلا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلاً. (As for him who is given his book in his right hand, they will read their book, and they will not be wronged so much as a thread.)

(٦٦) ** وَلَوْ نَشاءُ لَطَمَسْنا عَلَى أَعْيُنِهِمْ ** (66) And had We wished, We would have blotted out their eyes, We would have wiped away their eyes until they became smooth (or blotted out). فَاسْتَبَقُوا الصِّراطَ (and they would race to the path,) and they would race to the path which they were accustomed to traversing. فَأَنَّى يُبْصِرُونَ ** (then how would they see?) the path and the direction of travel, let alone anything else.

(٦٧) وَلَوْ نَشاءُ لَمَسَخْناهُمْ ** (And had We wished, We would have transformed them) by changing their forms and nullifying their powers عَلى مَكانَتِهِمْ ** (in their places) their place, such that they would be extinguished therein. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning, in the world (الدنيا). And it was recited (مَكانَاتِهِمْ). فَمَا اسْتَطاعُوا مُضِيًّا ** (then they would not be able to go ahead) a going forth (ذهاباً) وَلا يَرْجِعُونَ ** (nor return) nor a returning (رجوعاً), or they would not return from their denial.

(٦٨) وَمَنْ نُعَمِّرْهُ We prolong his life نُنَكِّسْهُ فِي الْخَلْقِ We turn him over in it, so his weakness continuously increases, and the structure of his body and his strengths diminish, contrary to what he was like at the beginning of his life. And it was read with takhfif (التخفيف). ** أَفَلا يَعْقِلُونَ ** (68) And whomsoever We grant a long life, We reverse him in creation. Will they not then apply reason? Indeed, whoever is capable of that is capable of effacement (الطمس) and transformation (المسخ), for it encompasses both of them and more, except that it occurs gradually. And it was read with the tā' (بالتاء).

** وَما عَلَّمْناهُ الشِّعْرَ (٦٩) ** (69) We have not taught him poetry, (The explanation is linked to the teaching of the Qur'an). It means that what We have revealed to Him is not related to the craft (صناعة) of poetry in any way—that is, it is not among the things sought by poets, such as appealing or repulsive imaginings (التخيّلات المرغّبة والمنفرّة) and the like, which have no reality (حقيقة) or basis (أصل). Rather, it is pure deception (تمويه محض), whether it is metered (موزوناً) or unmetered (غير موزون). ** وَما يَنْبَغِي لَهُ ** (and it is not befitting for him). This refers to this craft (هذه الصناعة).

Al-Qummi said: Quraysh used to say that what Muhammad was saying was poetry (شعر). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, refuted them. He said: And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله never spoke poetry (شعراً) at all.

I say: It is as if the intended meaning is that He did not speak poetic discourse (كلاماً شعريّاً), not that He did not speak metered discourse (كلاماً موزوناً). For the term al-shi'r (الشعر) is applied to both meanings collectively. This is why they considered the Qur'an poetry (شعراً), even though it is neither rhymed (مقفّى) nor metered (موزون).

It has been narrated in the Hadith: "Indeed, some poetry (الشعر) is wisdom (حكمة)," meaning metered speech (الكلام الموزون). And metered phrases (كلمات موزونة) have been transmitted from Him صلى الله عليه وآله, such as His saying:

انا النبيّ «صلى الله عليه وآله» لا كذب

انا ابن عبد المطّلب

And His saying:

هل أنت الّا إصبع دميت

وفي سبيل الله ما لقيت

And other such instances. And what the common people (العامّة) narrate—that He used to recite verses (الأبيات) incorrectly so that they would become unmetered (غير موزونة)—is not established (لم يثبت). If it were authentic (صحّ), perhaps He only did that so that they would not imagine that He was a poet and that His speech was poetic discourse (كلام شعريّ). For meter (الوزن) and rhyme (القافية) are not a defect (نقص) in speech. If they were a defect, the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام would not have employed them, and verses (الأبيات) have been widely transmitted (استفاض) from him, as well as from the rest of the Imams (ساير الأئمّة). Rather, the defect is in the poetic discourse (الكلام الشعريّ) itself.

He said in al-Majma': It has been authenticated that He صلى الله عليه وآله used to listen to poetry (الشعر) and encourage it. He said to Hassan ibn Thabit: "O Hassan, you shall remain supported by the Holy Spirit (روح القدس) as long as you defend us with your tongue."

** إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ ** (It is nothing but a reminder) (meaning: an admonition (عظة))

** وَقُرْآنٌ مُبِينٌ ** (and a manifest Qur’an) (meaning: a heavenly book (كتاب سماويّ) recited in the places of worship (المعابد)).

(٧٠) لِيُنْذِرَ ** (so that he may warn) And it was read with the tāʾ (لِتُنْذِرَ) مَنْ كانَ حَيًّا. (whoever is alive) In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): meaning, intelligent (عاقلاً). And al-Qummi: meaning, a believer (مؤمناً) with a living heart (حيّ القلب). And in its meaning, there is another narration that passed in Surat al-An'am regarding His saying: أَوَمَنْ كانَ مَيْتاً فَأَحْيَيْناهُ ** (Or is he who was dead and We gave him life) And the two meanings are similar. ** وَيَحِقَّ الْقَوْلُ ** (and that the word may be fulfilled), meaning, and the word of punishment becomes binding ** عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ ** (against the faithless), those who persist in disbelief (المصرّين على الكفر).

(٧١) أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا خَلَقْنا لَهُمْ مِمَّا عَمِلَتْ أَيْدِينا (71) Do they not see that We have created for them, of what Our hands have wrought, It was said: meaning, from what We undertook its creation, and none other than Us was able to bring it into existence. And the mention of the hands (الأيدي) and the attribution of the action to them is a metaphor that conveys an emphasis on the exclusivity and uniqueness in bringing them into existence. And al-Qummi (said): meaning, We created them by Our power (قوّتنا). livestock (أَنْعاماً). He specified them with mention due to what is in them of the wonders of creation (الفطرة) and the abundance of benefits. **فَهُمْ لَها مالِكُونَ ** (71) so they are their masters? They dispose of them through Our subjugation of them for their benefit.

(٧٢) وَذَلَّلْناها لَهُمْ ** (72) and We have subjected them to them meaning, We made them subservient to them, for indeed the camels, despite their strength and size, are led by a child. فَمِنْها رَكُوبُهُمْ ** and some of them are their mounts their ridden animals (مركوبهم). وَمِنْها يَأْكُلُونَ ** and some of them they eat. meaning, they eat its flesh. (٧٣) وَلَهُمْ فِيها مَنافِعُ ** (73) And there are benefits for them in them through what they earn by them, and from the hides (الجلود) and the wool (الأصواف) and the fur (الأوبار), وَمَشارِبُ (and drinks) from their milk. أَفَلا يَشْكُرُونَ ** Will they not then be grateful? for the favors (نعم) of Allah in that. (٧٤) وَاتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ آلِهَةً ** (74) They have taken gods besides Allah which they associate with Him in worship (العبادة). لَعَلَّهُمْ يُنْصَرُونَ ** so that they may be helped. hoping that they will help them. (٧٥) لا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُمْ جُنْدٌ مُحْضَرُونَ. (75) They cannot help them, and they are a host brought before them. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: The gods cannot provide them help, and they (the worshippers) are a host (جُنْدٌ) brought before the gods. It is said: meaning, they are prepared (معدّون) to guard them and defend them (الذب عنهم), or مُحْضَرُونَ ** (brought before them) means their trace (أثرهم) in the Fire (النار).

(٧٦) فَلا يَحْزُنْكَ قَوْلُهُمْ ** (76) So do not let their remarks grieve you. concerning Allah, through polytheism (الشرك) and atheism (الإلحاد), or concerning you, through denial (التكذيب) and vilification (التهجين). إِنَّا نَعْلَمُ ما يُسِرُّونَ وَما يُعْلِنُونَ (We indeed know what they keep secret and what they disclose), so We shall recompense them for it. And that is sufficient as a consolation for you. (٧٧) أَوَلَمْ يَرَ الْإِنْسانُ أَنَّا خَلَقْناهُ مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ فَإِذا هُوَ خَصِيمٌ مُبِينٌ ** (77) Has not man regarded that We created him from a drop of semen? Then behold, he is an open contender! Al-Qummi said: meaning, articulate (ناطق), knowledgeable (عالم), eloquent (بليغ). It is said: a second consolation, by minimizing what they say in their denial of the Resurrection (الحشر). (٧٨) وَضَرَبَ لَنا مَثَلاً ** (78) and he draws a parallel for Us, a strange matter, which is the negation of the ability to revive the dead. وَنَسِيَ خَلْقَهُ (and forgets his own creation), Our creation of him. قالَ مَنْ يُحْيِ الْعِظامَ وَهِيَ رَمِيمٌ ** (He says, 'Who will revive the bones when they have rotted away?') denying it and deeming it far-fetched. And al-Ramīm (الرّميم) is that which has decayed from the bones.

(٧٩) قُلْ يُحْيِيهَا الَّذِي أَنْشَأَها أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** (79) Say, 'He will revive them who produced them the first time.' For His power is as it was. وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ خَلْقٍ عَلِيمٌ ** (80) and He has knowledge of every creation. He knows the details of the created things, the manner of their creation, their fragmented and scattered parts—their origins (أصولها) and their divisions (فصولها), their locations, the way they are distinguished, and how some are joined to others.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: Ubayy ibn Khalaf came and took a decayed bone from a wall, then he crumbled it (فَفَتَّه), and then he said: "O Muhammad, **إِذا كُنَّا عِظاماً وَرُفاتاً أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ خَلْقاً ** (78) when we have become bones and dust, shall we be resurrected as a new creation?" So, [this verse] was revealed.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): a similar account.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, the spirit (الروح) remains in its place—the spirit of the righteous (المحسن) is in light and spaciousness, and the spirit of the evil-doer (المسيء) is in confinement and darkness. The body turns into dust, just as it was created from it. And whatever the beasts of prey and vermin cast out from their bellies of what they have eaten and torn apart—all of that is preserved in the dust with Him from Whom the weight of an atom is not hidden in the darknesses of the earth. He knows the number of things and their weight. And indeed, the dust of the spiritual beings (الرّوحانيين) is like the status of gold in the dust. When the time of the Resurrection (البعث) arrives, the earth is rained upon with the rain of revival (مطر النشور), and the earth swells. Then it is churned like the churning of the waterskin (السقاء), and the dust of mankind becomes like the fate of gold from the dust when it is washed with water, or like the foam (الزّبد) from milk when it is churned. Then the dust of every mold (قالب) gathers to its mold, and it moves by the permission of the All-Powerful God (الله القادر) to where the spirit is. Then the forms (الصّور) return by the permission of the Fashioner (المصوّر) to their original state, and the spirit enters into them. When he is thus complete, he does not deny anything of himself.

(٨٠) ** الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الشَّجَرِ الْأَخْضَرِ ناراً ** (80) (He) who made fire for you from the green tree, It was said that this is by rubbing the marwkh (١) against the ‘afār, and both of them are green, and water drips from them, yet fire is struck forth. Al-Qummi (said): The marwkh and the ‘afār are found in a region of the lands of the Arabs. When they wish to kindle a fire, they take from that tree, then they take a stick (عُوداً) and move it within it, and they kindle fire from it. فَإِذا أَنْتُمْ مِنْهُ تُوقِدُونَ and behold, you light fire from it. You do not doubt that it is fire that emerges from it.

footnote (١) المرخ is a tree that kindles fire quickly.

(٨١) أَوَلَيْسَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ** (81) Is not He who created the heavens and the earth despite the magnitude of their mass (جرمهما) and the greatness of their affair (شأنهما) بِقادِرٍ عَلى أَنْ يَخْلُقَ مِثْلَهُمْ ** capable of creating the likes of them? in smallness and insignificance. And it was recited (يَقْدِرُ). بَلى ** Yes, indeed! an answer from Allah وَهُوَ الْخَلَّاقُ الْعَلِيمُ ** He is indeed the All-creator, the All-knowing. abundant in created things (المخلوقات) and known things (المعلومات).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): As for disputation (الجدال) in the best manner (بالتي هي أحسن), it is what Allah commanded His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to dispute with those who denied the Resurrection after death and His bringing them back to life. So He said, relating from him (the denier): وَضَرَبَ لَنا مَثَلاً وَنَسِيَ خَلْقَهُ ** and he draws a parallel for Us, and forgets his own creation. the Ayah. So He desired of His Prophet (peace be upon him) that he dispute with the one who spoke falsehood (المبطل) and said: "How is it permissible (يجور) that these bones be resurrected while they are decayed (رميم)?" He said: قُلْ يُحْيِيهَا الَّذِي أَنْشَأَها أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** Say, 'He will revive them who produced them the first time.' Is He Who originated it from nothing (لا من شيء) incapable of restoring it after it has decayed? Rather, its origination is more difficult in your view than its restoration. Then He said: الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الشَّجَرِ الْأَخْضَرِ ناراً ** He who made for you fire from the green tree, meaning: when He conceals the hot fire within the green, moist tree, and then extracts it, He makes known to you that He is more capable of restoring that which has decayed. Then He said: أَوَلَيْسَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِقادِرٍ ** Is not He who created the heavens and the earth capable the Ayah. Meaning: If the creation of the heavens and the earth is greater and further removed in your imaginations (أوهامكم) and your estimation (قدركم)—that you should deem Him capable of it—than the restoration of the decayed, then how did you permit (جوّزتم) from Allah the creation of this thing which is more wondrous (الأعجب) in your view and more difficult (الأصعب) for you, yet you did not permit from Him that which is easier (أسهل) in your view, namely, the restoration of the decayed?

(٨٢) إِنَّما أَمْرُهُ His affair (شأنه) is only إِذا أَرادَ شَيْئاً أَنْ يَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ Be formed (تكوّن) ** فَيَكُونُ ** So it is/exists, meaning it comes into being (يحدث). And it was recited (وقرئ) with the accusative case (بالنّصب) [i.e., فَيَكُونَ]. And this is a similitude (تمثيل) for the effect of His power (قدرته) upon what He wills (مراده), likened to the command (أمر) of the one who is obeyed (المطاع) to the one who obeys (للمطيع) in the realization of the commanded thing (المأمور), without refusal (امتناع), delay (توقّف), or need (افتقار) for engaging in action (مزاولة عمل) or using a tool (استعمال آلة), thereby cutting off the source of doubt (قطعاً لمادّة الشّبهة).

** إِنَّما أَمْرُهُ إِذا أَرادَ شَيْئاً أَنْ يَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ ** (All His command is that, when He wills a thing, He just says to it, ‘Be,’ and it is.)

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): كُنْ ** (Be) is an act of creation (صنع) from Him, and that by which the created thing (المصنوع) comes into being.

And in Nahj al-Balagha: His speech (Exalted is He) is only an action (فعل) created (أنشأه) by Him. He (peace be upon him) said: He speaks (يقول) but does not utter words (يلفظ), and He wills (يريد) but does not conceal [thoughts] (يضمر). And He (peace be upon him) said: He wills (يريد) without effort (همّة). Other narrations (أخبار أخر) concerning this meaning have preceded in Surat al-Baqarah and elsewhere.

And al-Qummi said: His treasures (خزائنه) are in the Kaf (الكاف) and the Nun (النّون) [i.e., the letters forming كُنْ].

(٨٣) فَسُبْحانَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ مَلَكُوتُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** (83) So immaculate is He in whose hand is the dominion of all things,**

A declaration of His purity from what they attributed to Him, and an expression of astonishment at what they said about Him. And the dominion (مَلَكُوتُ) of all things is that by which that thing subsists, from the world of spirits (عالم الأرواح) and angels (الملائكة).

وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ ** and to Him you shall be brought back.**

A promise and a threat for those who affirm and those who deny. And it was recited with the opening of the tā (بفتح التاء).

( A Narrative Discussion )

In Thawab al-A'mal (في ثواب الأعمال), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Yā Sīn (يس) once in his lifetime, Allah writes for him, for every creature in the world, and for every creature in the Hereafter, and in the heaven, two million (ألفي الف) good deeds for each one, and He erases from him the like of that (in bad deeds). And poverty shall not afflict him, nor distress (عزم), nor destruction (هدم), nor fatigue (نصب), nor madness (جنون), nor leprosy (جذام), nor whispering (وسواس), nor any disease that harms him. And Allah alleviates for him the agonies of death (سكرات الموت) and its terrors, and He takes charge of seizing his soul. And he will be among those for whom Allah guarantees abundance (السّعة) in his livelihood, joy upon meeting Him, and satisfaction with the reward in his Hereafter. And Allah says to all the angels, those in the heavens and those on the earth: 'I am pleased with so-and-so, so seek forgiveness for him.'

And in it (Thawab al-A'mal) and in al-Majma' (وفي المجمع), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That for everything there is a heart, and the heart of the Quran is Yā Sīn (يس). (He mentioned) the hadith, and He mentioned therein a great reward for its recitation.
